description: Create new skills, review and improve existing skills, and diagnose triggering problems. Use when building a skill from scratch, turning a workflow into a reusable skill, reviewing a SKILL.md for spec violations, fixing a skill that loads at the wrong times, or iterating on a skill with test-driven discipline. Make sure to use this skill whenever the user mentions creating, editing, reviewing, packaging, or improving an agent skill, even if they don't use the word "skill" explicitly. Do NOT use for general Python coding, building MCP servers, writing documentation unrelated to skills, or testing non-skill software — use skill-evaluator for running evals on an existing skill.

results.query: my skill called recipe-helper never loads when users ask things like 'help me plan meals for next week' or 'what should I cook this weekend'. The current description is: 'Finds and adapts recipes for dietary restrictions.' How do I fix the description so it triggers for those queries?

Can you review this SKILL.md for me and tell me everything that's wrong with it?

---
name: My_SQLHelper
description: helps with SQL queries and database stuff.
metadata:
  version: 1.0
---

# SQL Helper

This skill helps you write SQL. Just ask me about SQL and I'll help.

I want to build a new skill for my Claude agent that helps with writing SQL queries. It should check for missing WHERE clauses on UPDATE statements, suggest indexes, and format output as a code review. Walk me through creating it from scratch including the SKILL.md and any scripts.
What files and folders do I need to create for a new skill called log-analyzer that will help triage Kubernetes pod crash logs? Show me every file with what goes in each one, including frontmatter.
I want to turn the workflow we just built together for triaging Kubernetes pod crashes into a reusable skill that other agents can use. How should I package it up?
How do I write a SKILL.md description field that reliably triggers for the right user queries and doesn't load when it shouldn't? Walk me through the process with an example.
my skill-evaluator skill keeps loading whenever users ask about testing Python code, even though it should only be for agent skills. How do I add negative triggers to stop that?
I want to improve an existing skill that's producing inconsistent outputs. The instructions section is vague and agents keep doing different things each time. Can you help me refactor it?
I need to package my pdf-extractor skill into a .skill file so I can share it with my team. What's the right approach and what tool do I use?

my skill script crashes in CI with 'EOFError: EOF when reading a line' — the deploy script prompts the user to pick staging or production but that breaks in automated pipelines. How do I fix this following skill best practices?

I have a Python data pipeline script that reads user input interactively but I need it to also work headlessly in GitHub Actions. Refactor this function so it handles both cases: def get_target_env(): return input('Deploy to staging or prod? ')
write a Python function that safely wraps input() so it doesn't crash when stdin is closed
how do I set up a CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions for a Python project that runs pytest on every PR?
write unit tests for this Python module that parses CSV files and returns a list of dicts
explain what YAML frontmatter is and how it's parsed in markdown files
how do I write a good README for an open source Python library so developers know how to install and use it?
create a markdown project README template for a Python CLI tool with installation, usage, and contributing sections
I need to handle KeyboardInterrupt gracefully in my Python CLI so it prints a clean exit message instead of a traceback
what's the best way to structure a Python project that has scripts, tests, config files, and a CLI entrypoint?
my subprocess is hanging because it's waiting for stdin input that never comes in my non-interactive shell script. How do I fix this?
